# Climbo 45-Min Training -- Sales & Coaching Summary

**Source:** YouTube video `tg2KXcR_RRg` (auto-generated English transcript)

---

## 1. Sales Methodology Taught

### Problem-First Selling
The entire pitch framework is built around leading with the prospect's pain before ever mentioning the product:

1. **Identify the universal problem** -- local businesses have too few positive reviews, which causes low Google/AI visibility, which means competitors capture the calls, bookings, and walk-ins.
2. **Agitate** -- "They are not sleeping at night for this problem... their competitors are getting all the calls, all the bookings."
3. **Present the solution** -- an automated "Review Marketing Flywheel" (Climbo's white-label SaaS) that collects reviews, boosts SEO, posts social proof, and generates an AI-optimized website/blog.
4. **Prove with social proof** -- share partner success stories (Wayne: $20K/mo; Martin: 5 restaurants in 1 week; Anna: first 3 pitches all said yes; Noa: 8 clients in 7 days door-to-door).
5. **Close with ROI visibility** -- show the dashboard so the prospect sees more impressions, calls, direction requests, and website clicks month over month.

### Sell Outcomes, Not Features
> "Local business owners don't buy features, they don't buy software, they don't buy platforms, they don't buy dashboards -- they buy outcomes."

The training repeatedly emphasizes translating every feature into a concrete business result (more calls, more bookings, more foot traffic).

### Recurring-Revenue Framing
The pitch explicitly contrasts a one-time project (website, ad campaign) with a monthly subscription that the client *cannot afford to cancel* because stopping the system means losing ranking momentum.

---

## 2. Pricing Advice

| Element | Guidance |
|---|---|
| **Monthly range** | $100 -- $300/mo per client (entry); up to $400 -- $600/mo for medium-sized businesses |
| **Tiered plans** | Bronze $99/mo, Silver $149/mo, Gold $199/mo (example given in demo) |
| **Per-location pricing** | Charge per Google Business Profile location -- multi-location clients pay per location |
| **Annual option** | Offer monthly *or* annual billing; Martin closed 5 restaurants on annual plans |
| **Free trial** | 14 days, with or without credit card required -- used as a foot-in-the-door tactic |
| **Climbo cost to the partner** | Fixed $99/mo subscription, unlimited client seats; currently 0% revenue commission (future: 10% commission, locked at sign-up rate) |
| **Stripe integration** | Payments flow through the partner's own Stripe account; weekly automated payouts to bank |

### Revenue Math Presented
- 10 clients x $300/mo = **$3,000 MRR** within ~6 months
- Wayne (Australia): 3 years in, **$20K+/mo** recurring
- Gary (solopreneur, full-time): **$5,000 MRR**
- Noa (week 1, door-to-door): 8 clients x $150/mo = **$1,200 MRR** immediately

---

## 3. Prospecting Tactics

### Target Profile
- **Who:** Any small local business within ~5 km of where you live -- restaurants, gyms, salons, clinics, mechanics, barber shops, dentists, lawyers, accountants.
- **Why them:** Every one of them needs more reviews, more visibility, more customers. Even businesses with thousands of reviews need *consistent new reviews every week* or Google drops their ranking.

### Channels
| Tactic | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Door-to-door / walk-in** | Go physically into the business, show them the problem on their phone (low review count, poor ranking), present the solution. Noa closed 8 clients in 7 days this way. |
| **Warm network** | Start with a business owner you already know (friend's barber shop, local restaurant). Create a case study, then get referrals. |
| **Network / referral effect** | Once one business in a category signs up, competitors in the same area feel the urge to sign up too. "You create a network effect in your area." |
| **Professional positioning** | Present yourself as a "local partner" -- not a remote marketing agency. Emphasize physical proximity, trust, and caring about their results. |

### First-Client Playbook
1. Pick someone you already know.
2. Offer the free trial (14 days).
3. Set up the review collection process (QR code is easiest).
4. Show them early results on the dashboard.
5. Convert to paid. Use the case study to approach their competitors and adjacent businesses.

---

## 4. Objection Handling (Implicit)

The training does not have a formal objection-handling section, but addresses common objections pre-emptively:

| Likely Objection | Pre-emptive Counter in the Training |
|---|---|
| "I already have reviews" | Even businesses with thousands of reviews need *new* reviews every week; Google penalizes inconsistency. |
| "I don't need another marketing agency" | This is not a complicated agency. It is a simple, automated system with a local partner they trust. |
| "It's too expensive" | Frame it as ROI: more calls, more direction requests, more bookings. The dashboard proves the return every month. |
| "I can do this myself" | The system is automated; three AI agents work in the background. They don't have to lift a finger. |
| "I tried something before and it didn't work" | Social proof from 2,000+ entrepreneurs using the system since 2022. Specific testimonials with dollar amounts. |
| "I want to think about it" | Offer a free trial (14 days, no credit card required option) to remove risk. |

---

## 5. Feature Positioning

Every feature is framed as a business outcome, not a technical capability:

### Review Marketing Flywheel (Core Framework)
**Reviews -> Trust -> Visibility -> Customers -> More Reviews** (self-reinforcing loop)

### Three AI Agents
| Agent | What It Does | How It Is Positioned to the Prospect |
|---|---|---|
| **SEO Agent** | Auto-replies to every review using local SEO keywords (e.g., "dentist in Austin") | "Your business ranks higher in Google Maps because every review reply reinforces your keyword." |
| **Social Agent** | Picks best reviews, designs social-proof posts, publishes to Facebook/Instagram/TikTok with captions | "You get professional social media content every week without lifting a finger -- and it is the most powerful content: real customer proof." |
| **GEO Agent** | Auto-generates and maintains a website + blog with schema markup, optimized for AI search (ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity) | "When someone asks AI for the best dentist in Austin, your business gets recommended because our system feeds the AI the right content." |

### Review Collection Methods (3 options, set up once)
1. **QR codes in-location** -- printed cards/table tents; employee reward leaderboard to gamify collection.
2. **SMS / Email / WhatsApp campaigns** -- quick single requests or mass CSV upload campaigns with automatic reminders.
3. **CRM automation** -- connect via Google Sheets or Zapier; auto-send review request after configurable delay.

### Dashboard & Retention Hooks
- **Review dashboard** -- average rating trend, reviews needed to reach next rating milestone (gamification).
- **AI sentiment analysis** -- actionable improvement suggestions derived from review text.
- **Google Maps ranking tracker** -- visual grid showing position vs. competitors.
- **AI ranking tracker** -- shows position in ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity results.
- **Performance metrics** -- impressions, call clicks, direction requests, website clicks, keyword traffic -- all compared to previous month.

### White-Label / Back Office
- Full brand customization (logo, colors, fonts, domain, AI agent names/avatars).
- Stripe-integrated payment gateway; partner sets their own prices.
- Client management dashboard with subscription status, plan details, billing portal.
- Automated lifecycle emails (welcome, trial ending, payment success, payment failure).

---

## 6. Specific Scripts & Frameworks

### The "Problem Slide" Script (Opening Pitch)
> "Many businesses -- almost all the businesses in your area: restaurants, gyms, salons, clinics, mechanics, barber shops -- they are struggling with too few positive reviews. For this reason they have low Google and AI visibility... their competitors are getting all the calls, all the bookings, all the customers. They are leaving money on the table."

### The "Flywheel" Elevator Pitch
> "It's an AI system designed to turn reviews into visibility, visibility into customers, and customers into more reviews."

### The "Outcome, Not Feature" Reframe
> "They don't buy features, they don't buy software, they don't buy platforms, they don't buy dashboards -- they buy outcomes: more people finding them, more people calling them, more people choosing them."

### The Recurring Revenue Pitch
> "You're not selling a one-time project. You're not selling a website. You're selling a system they need every month. And for this reason, they will pay you from $100 to $300 per month."

### The Network Effect Pitch
> "Once a local business starts using the software, their competitors also feel the urge to sign up."

### The Urgency Close (for Climbo sign-up)
> "Right now we are still in the early bird phase. We are not charging any commission... for sure in the future we're going to set up a 10% commission... if you sign up right now you get this commission [0%] forever."

---

## Key Takeaways

1. **The entire sales process is problem-led, not product-led.** Every interaction starts by showing the prospect their review gap and ranking weakness.
2. **Retention is engineered into the product.** Gamified dashboards, ranking trackers, and visible month-over-month improvement make cancellation psychologically difficult.
3. **The partner's only job is sales and onboarding.** Everything else (technology, AI agents, billing, payouts) is handled by the platform.
4. **Door-to-door and warm-network prospecting are the primary channels.** No ad spend, no cold email -- physical presence and local trust are the differentiators.
5. **Pricing is positioned as affordable relative to ROI.** $100-300/mo is framed as trivially cheap compared to the additional customers the business gains.
6. **Free trial (14 days) is the standard foot-in-the-door.** Results appear within the trial window, making conversion natural.
